summary: Audience recording captures a high‑energy Buffalo show with standout Bertha/Greatest Story/Cold Rain & Snow suite and an uplifting U.S. Blues encore.
review: The performance is praised for its upbeat vibe, tight setlist flow and several highlight moments – notably the Bertha > Greatest Story > Cold Rain and Snow sequence, Touch > Man Smart, Terrapin Station and a soaring U.S. Blues finale. The audience‑sourced tape is considered decent; reviewers wish it were an SBD but find it listenable enough to enjoy the show.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently strong 4‑5 star reviews highlight a solid, energetic performance; recording is an audience tape that’s deemed good but not exceptional.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers note it is an audience (AUD) source and would give a higher rating if it were SBD; no major drop‑outs or distortion reported, overall acceptable clarity.

standout_songs: Man Smart (House of the Rising Sun)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with a Bertha‑Greatest Story‑Cold Rain suite; second set maintains momentum with Touch > Man Smart and moves into reflective ballads before ending on an uplifting U.S. Blues encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for sounding better in ’89, setting the mood, and missed by fans.
